author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session focused on finalizing Workshop 1 materials for a DHS Consulting engagement called 'Building Bridges.' The Presenter Guide (BB_W1_Presenter_Guide.md) was edited multiple times to address QA issues found in the v3 slide deck. A v3 QA pass identified 9 issues; 8 were resolved including correcting an admin cap from <20% to <10% on slide 28. The one outstanding issue was slide 16 wording. Both the Presenter Guide and Chat Drops were reconverted to PDF. A file readiness check confirmed all pre-session and chat drop files were present. The user explicitly instructed not to edit the PPTX file — only the facilitator markdown docs were modified. A day-of pre-workshop email (morning send with join link and three attachments) was drafted to close out session prep.
problems: v3 QA identified 9 issues across the slide deck and facilitator documents
Admin cap percentage was incorrect on slide 28 (<20% instead of <10%)
Slide 16 wording was still incorrect after the QA pass
Files needed to be reconverted to PDF after markdown edits
approaches: Edited BB_W1_Presenter_Guide.md directly using the Edit tool to address QA findings
Used python-pptx to read PPTX slide content without modifying the file
Ran a bash script to reconvert Presenter Guide and Chat Drops to PDF
Ran a file readiness check to verify all pre-session and chat drop files existed
results: 8 of 9 QA issues resolved in v3
Slide 28 admin cap corrected from <20% to <10%
All chat drop and pre-session send files confirmed READY
Presenter Guide and Chat Drops successfully reconverted to PDF
Day-of pre-workshop email drafted with subject 'Building Bridges is today: join link + files'

decisions.decision: Do not edit the PPTX file — only read it with python-pptx and apply changes to facilitator markdown docs

open_questions: What is the correct wording for slide 16 and who will fix it?
What are the actual TIME and LINK values for the morning email?
Was the session fully done after the email draft or were there remaining items?
